mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand as real borrowing costs rise, compressing forward valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following unemployment spikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not statistically significant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL inflation) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, signaling economic strength that supports positive equity valuations and 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 2% month-over-month and HOUST > 1200k units annualized
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness and negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100k and UNRATE unchanged or rising
regime_hint: labor market transition from expansion to contraction
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or joint coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.5% annualized while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is contained below 3.5% year-over-year, indicating a Goldilocks regime of sustainable growth without overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: Goldilocks expansion with moderate growth and controlled inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in Goldilocks regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or average forward return differential versus other regimes is < 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds the year-over-year CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) by more than 1.5 percentage points, real rates are restrictive, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 20-day forward returns as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: restrictive real rate environment during monetary tightening
failure_condition: Real rate spread signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150k per month, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 1.0% and PAYEMS growth > 150k
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side and labor expansion in mid-cycle
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in predictive regression.
